query_text: death
The patient's vital biological functions ceased irreversibly.
What does death mean for a person and for those who remain?
The family has been told that death is near and must decide how to accompany the patient.
Death ends earthly life but is faced with hope in resurrection and communion with God.
In Catholic teaching death ends earthly life but is faced with hope in resurrection and communion with God.
Death is the biological end of earthly life and is also faced with prayer and hope in resurrection.
grief
She is grieving after the death of her mother.
She is trying to understand what her grief means and how to live with it.
Since the death she can barely sleep work or speak to anyone.
In mourning sorrow is brought to God in prayer and held with hope in the resurrection.
In Catholic life sorrow in mourning is brought to God in prayer and held with hope in the resurrection.
Grief may require psychological care while also being held in prayer and hope in the resurrection.
euthanasia
The patient requested medical assistance to end life because of unbearable suffering.
Is ending a patient's life an act of mercy or a violation of dignity?
A terminally ill patient in severe pain asks the clinical team to help end life.
Compassionate care should relieve suffering while refusing the intentional ending of innocent human life.
In Catholic teaching compassionate care should relieve suffering while refusing the intentional ending of innocent human life.
Palliative medicine can relieve severe suffering while respecting the person's dignity and refusing intentional killing.
grace
She moved across the stage with grace and elegance.
He spoke about receiving grace during a difficult time.
Human beings receive an unearned divine gift that heals and draws them into life with God.
In Catholic teaching grace is the unearned divine gift that heals and draws human beings into life with God.
judgment after death
Many religions describe a judgment after death in which deeds are evaluated.
What might judgment after death mean for the way a person has lived?
After death each person faces judgment before God where the truth of life is revealed in mercy and justice.
In Catholic teaching after death each person faces particular judgment before God where the truth of life is revealed in mercy and justice.
Religions speak broadly of afterlife judgment while Catholic doctrine describes a particular judgment before God.
